issue.title: Selected Tab Text Not Visible
issue.description: User reports they still cannot see the text on the selected tab pill, despite multiple attempts to fix the styling.

element.expected-appearance: White text (#ffffff) on dark slate background (#0f172a)
element.actual-appearance: User reports text is not visible

hypothesis.title: CSS Specificity Conflict
hypothesis.details: Tailwind classes may be overridden by more specific CSS rules. The base button classes include "text-slate-600" which appears earlier in the class string and might be overriding "text-white".
hypothesis.evidence: - All pills have same base classes before active classes appended - text-slate-600 appears in base template (line 5449) - text-white is added via JS after the fact
hypothesis.test: Inspect element in browser DevTools and check: 1. Computed color value for the text 2. Which CSS rule is winning (check strikethrough rules) 3. Whether text-white class is even in the final CSS bundle

hypothesis.title: Tailwind CSS Bundle Missing Classes
hypothesis.details: The built Tailwind CSS (css/tailwind.build.css) may not include the active state classes because they weren't in the HTML at build time (they're added dynamically by JavaScript).
hypothesis.evidence: - Classes are added via JS: cfg.activeClasses (line 5273) - Tailwind scans HTML at build time for class usage - Dynamic classes may not be included in final bundle
hypothesis.test: 1. Search css/tailwind.build.css for ".bg-slate-900" 2. Search for ".text-white" 3. Search for ".font-semibold" 4. If missing, add safelist to tailwind.config.js

hypothesis.title: Font Color Inheritance Override
hypothesis.details: Parent or global styles may be setting color via !important or through a more specific selector.
hypothesis.evidence: - #mini-stepper has various styles (line 591-605) - .step base styles exist in CSS

solution.implementation: Add to tailwind.config.js: ``` safelist: [ 'bg-slate-900', 'text-white', 'font-semibold', 'scale-105', 'shadow-md' ] ``` Then rebuild: npm run build:css

solution.action: Use Inline Styles for Active State

issue.title: Toolbar Still Showing Glitchy Load Behavior
issue.description: Despite implementing opacity fade-in, the toolbar still appears glitchy during page load with elements jumping around.

element.timing: Opacity set to 1 via requestAnimationFrame after initializeTopToolbar()

hypothesis.title: Reparenting Happens After Fade-In
hypothesis.details: The reparent() function moves DOM elements around, causing visible layout shifts even though opacity transition is smooth. The fade-in happens too early, before layout is stable.
hypothesis.evidence: - reparent() calls happen in initializeTopToolbar() (lines 1813-1826) - Fade-in happens immediately after via requestAnimationFrame - DOM elements are physically moved, triggering reflow
hypothesis.test: Open DevTools Performance tab: 1. Record page load 2. Look for Layout/Reflow events 3. Check if they occur after paint

hypothesis.title: Content Not Pre-Positioned
hypothesis.details: Elements are "pre-populated" in HTML but still need to be rearranged via JS. This causes visible movement even during fade-in transition.
hypothesis.evidence: - Elements exist in tbLeft/tbCenter/tbRight initially - reparent() moves them to different containers - Movement happens while opacity transitions from 0 to 1
hypothesis.test: Remove all reparent() calls temporarily and see if glitching stops. If yes, the issue is the DOM manipulation.

hypothesis.title: Timing Race Condition
hypothesis.details: requestAnimationFrame fires before browser has finished layout calculations from the reparenting operations.
hypothesis.evidence: - Single rAF call may not be sufficient - Browser needs time to calculate final layout - Opacity change happens in same frame as layout

solution.action: Hide Toolbar Container, Not Just Opacity

solution.implementation:
Eliminate reparent() calls by rendering elements in their final positions from the start. This requires restructuring the HTML to match the desired layout before any JS runs.
Most thorough fix but requires significant HTML changes.

command.title: Check if Tailwind classes exist in bundle
Browser DevTools Console Command
Test Layout Timing

step.title: Add Inline Style Fallback for Pills
step.reason: Guaranteed to work regardless of CSS bundle

step.title: Fix Toolbar Timing
step.test: Reload page multiple times, observe toolbar load

step.title: Add CSS !important Rule as Safety Net
step.reason: Ensures pills work even if JS fails
